author: nero_eth
content:
For visibility, please check out this PR here:
https://github.com/ethereum/pm/issues/2115#issuecomment-4709317795

We want to decide on it on tomorrow's ACD so would be great if clients are prepared.

author: m.kalinin
content: one of the problems of introducing *any* queue mechanism for builder deposits was the risk of large builders to gatekeep. doesn't this issue exist with the system contract?

author: pk910
content: the exponential fee makes it very expensive to block the deposit queue,    and builders can still buy in for more if they can afford

author: m.kalinin
content: i think a builder can keep the fee prohibitive for others by keeping excess at a certain level

author: pk910
content: yea,  but another builder can always pay more to get a spot in the queue,  and with a dequeue rate of 256deposits per slot, it won'T even take a long time

author: m.kalinin
content: I see. Deposit processing will happen at a much higher rate than depositing, right? Then probably we don't have such an issue

author: potuz
content: Yeah,  the cost for the censoring builder is much higher than four the censored one
